title: Defender quarantined malware on endpoint

servicenow_seed.short_description: [TRAINING] Defender quarantined a malicious file on user laptop
servicenow_seed.description: Defender for Endpoint quarantined a file on user's laptop. Alert ID raised; device automatically isolated by EDR policy.

correct_steps: Verify caller and confirm the device is the one in the alert
Do NOT release the device from isolation
Capture alert ID, file path, file hash if available
Ask the user what they were doing when the file landed (download, email, USB?)
Escalate to Security Operations with the alert ID and user statement
rationale: An EDR isolation is a containment action. Service Desk supports the user and gathers context; SecOps decides when to release isolation.
hints: Never release isolation without SecOps approval.
User context (where the file came from) is gold for SecOps.
learning_objectives: Treat EDR isolation as a containment success, not a problem to undo

tool_clues.security: Defender alert MD-44129 - 'Trojan:Win32/Wacatac' quarantined at C:\Users\bob\Downloads\invoice.docm.exe. Device isolated.
tool_clues.endpoint: Last process: Outlook attachment opened 14 minutes before quarantine.
